texto: Do not stand at my grave and weep, I am not there, I do not sleep. I am in a thousand winds that blow, I am the softly falling snow. I am the gentle showers of rain, I am the fields of ripening grain. I am in the morning hush, I am in the graceful rush Of beautiful birds in circling flight, I am the starshine of the night. I am in the flowers that bloom, I am in a quiet room. I am in the birds that sing, I am in each lovely thing. Do not stand at my grave bereft I am not there. I have not left.
explicacao: Citação de Mary Elizabeth Frye sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Do not stand at my grave and weep, I am not there, I do not sleep. I am in a thousand winds that …” articula amor, death, grave em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e bom-dia.
